# SentoVox — European Visitor Research for Performing Arts Venues

## What is SentoVox?
SentoVox is a free European study that helps performing arts venues understand their audiences. It is developed by Sento Group BV (Belgium) and supported by a consortium of researchers at seven European universities, together with sector partners such as ENCC, VSCD and Cult.

## What does SentoVox do?
SentoVox provides theatres, concert halls and cultural centres with a standardised, scientifically grounded visitor survey covering visitor experience, programming evaluation, the Impact Compass Model, infrastructure, communication, audience profile, NPS, non-visitor research and economic impact. Standard questions can be supplemented with optional local questions per venue.

## Who uses SentoVox?
130+ cultural venues across Europe, with 60,000+ visitors having taken part in the survey. The study is run as a single coordinated edition with academic oversight.

## Key facts
- Free for all venues — optional paid plans for deeper reporting
- European study for performing arts venues (theatres, concert halls, cultural centres)
- Multilingual survey: English, Dutch, French, German, Italian, Spanish
- GDPR compliant - fully anonymous responses, no personal data collected
- Backed by 7 European universities
- Includes NPS (Net Promoter Score) and the Impact Compass Model
- World Land Trust partnership: 1 m2 of endangered rainforest preserved per completed survey
- Sector partners: ENCC, VSCD, Cult and other national associations

## Participation
- Registration open until 30 June 2026
- Research period runs until 31 October 2026
- Participation fee: EUR 625 to EUR 1,750, depending on visitor volume
- Group discounts for cities/regions with multiple venues

## Contact
- General: info@sentovox.eu
- Website: https://sentovox.eu
